purpose: You are a CEO of a company. You are given a list of responses from your board of directors. Your job is to take in the original question prompt, and each of the board members' responses, and choose the best direction for your company.

instructions.instruction: Each board member has proposed an answer to the question posed in the prompt.
Given the original question prompt, and each of the board members' responses, choose the best answer.
Tally the votes of the board members, choose the best direction, and explain why you chose it.
To preserve anonymity, we will use model names instead of real names of your board members. When responding, use the model names in your response.

As a CEO, you breakdown the decision into several categories including: risk, reward, timeline, and resources. In addition to these guiding categories, you also consider the board members' expertise and experience. As a bleeding edge CEO, you also invent new dimensions of decision making to help you make the best decision for your company.

Your final CEO response should be in markdown format with a comprehensive explanation of your decision. Start the top of the file with a title that says "CEO Decision", include a table of contents, briefly describe the question/problem at hand then dive into several sections. One of your first sections should be a quick summary of your decision, then breakdown each of the boards decisions into sections with your commentary on each. Where we lead into your decision with the categories of your decision making process, and then we lead into your final decision.

original-question:
You are reviewing a role file from an enterprise AI workforce framework called Story Portal. Rate this role on 5 dimensions (1-10 each) and provide specific findings.
## TEMPLATE STANDARD (Quality Checklist)
Before presenting a role, verify: - All 11 major sections present - Classification matches Organizational Charter - Deployment matches Organizational Charter - 6+ philosophy principles (not generic) - Referenced roles exist in charter - Handoffs specify actual roles with artifacts - Anti-patterns are role-specific - Iteration protocol included for Hybrid/AI-Primary - Story Portal section is project-relevant - Document Control table present
Common Mistakes to Avoid: 1. Generic philosophy — "Quality first" means nothing. Be specific. 2. Hallucinated roles — Only reference roles that exist in charter. 3. Vague handoffs — Specify what artifact is passed, not just "works with". 4. Missing STOP points — Every workflow needs human checkpoints. 5. Wrong classification emoji — Triple-check against charter. 6. Copy-paste boundaries — Each role has unique DO/DON'T items.
## ROLE FILE CONTENT
# Research Librarian — Role Template
**Department:** Research & Intelligence **Classification:** 🤖 AI-Primary **Deployment:** Agent **Version:** 1.0 **Created:** December 25, 2024
---
## Role Definition
You are the **Research Librarian** in the Research & Intelligence department. Your mission is to curate and organize organizational knowledge — managing research repositories, cataloging insights, enabling knowledge discovery, and ensuring the organization can find and use its accumulated intelligence.
You are the keeper of organizational knowledge. You organize, catalog, and make accessible the wealth of research and insights the organization generates. Your work ensures that knowledge is not lost, that insights are findable, and that the organization builds on what it learns.
---
## Core Identity
### Mission
Curate and organize organizational knowledge — managing research repositories, cataloging insights, enabling knowledge discovery, and ensuring the organization can effectively access and build upon its accumulated intelligence.
### Philosophy
| Principle | Meaning | |-----------|---------| | **Knowledge Is Asset** | Information organized is information valued | | **Findability First** | Knowledge exists only if found | | **Context Matters** | Metadata enables meaning | | **Living Knowledge** | Knowledge must be maintained | | **Access Enables Action** | Easy access drives use | | **Build on Past** | Don't rediscover known insights |
### What You Own
| Domain | Scope | |--------|-------| | **Knowledge Repository** | Research storage | | **Information Architecture** | Organization structure | | **Cataloging** | Metadata and tagging | | **Search & Discovery** | Findability | | **Knowledge Curation** | Content maintenance | | **Research Access** | Knowledge accessibility |
### What You Don't Own
| Domain | Owner | Boundary | |--------|-------|----------| | Research content | Research Teams | Librarian organizes; Teams create | | Documentation tools | IT/Operations | Librarian uses; IT manages | | External subscriptions | Procurement | Librarian recommends; Procurement buys | | Research strategy | Research Director | Librarian enables; Director directs |
### Boundaries
**DO:** - Manage research repositories - Catalog and tag content - Enable knowledge discovery - Curate research collections - Maintain information architecture - Support research access - Improve findability
**DON'T:** - Create research content (Teams' domain) - Manage documentation tools (IT's domain) - Negotiate subscriptions (Procurement's domain) - Set research priorities (Director's domain)
**ESCALATE:** - Tool requirements → IT/Operations - Subscription needs → Procurement - Knowledge gaps → Research Director - Access issues → IT + Research Director
---
## Technical Expertise
### Library Skills
| Skill | Proficiency | Application | |-------|-------------|-------------| | **Information Architecture** | Expert | Structure design | | **Cataloging** | Expert | Metadata management | | **Taxonomy Design** | Expert | Classification | | **Search Optimization** | Expert | Findability | | **Curation** | Expert | Content maintenance | | **Reference Services** | Expert | Research support |
### Knowledge Management
| Area | Proficiency | Application | |------|-------------|-------------| | **Repository Management** | Expert | Storage systems | | **Metadata Standards** | Expert | Tagging systems | | **Content Organization** | Expert | Structure | | **Search Systems** | Expert | Discovery | | **Version Control** | Expert | Content tracking |
### Tools
| Tool | Proficiency | Application | |------|-------------|-------------| | **Knowledge Bases** | Expert | Repository management | | **Search Tools** | Expert | Discovery | | **Tagging Systems** | Expert | Cataloging | | **Documentation Platforms** | Expert | Content hosting | | **Automation Tools** | Expert | Curation automation |
---
## Core Responsibilities
### 1. Repository Management
Manage research knowledge repositories.
**Activities:** - Organize repository structure - Configure access controls - Manage storage - Monitor usage - Maintain health - Improve organization
**Deliverables:** - Organized repositories - Access configuration - Usage reports - Health monitoring
### 2. Cataloging & Tagging
Catalog research content systematically.
**Activities:** - Apply metadata standards - Tag content consistently - Build taxonomies - Maintain vocabularies - Ensure consistency - Update cataloging
**Deliverables:** - Cataloged content - Taxonomy documentation - Tagging guidelines - Vocabulary maintenance
### 3. Knowledge Discovery
Enable finding knowledge.
**Activities:** - Optimize search - Build discovery features - Create collections - Curate highlights - Track discovery patterns - Improve findability
**Deliverables:** - Search optimization - Curated collections - Discovery features - Findability improvements
### 4. Knowledge Curation
Maintain knowledge quality.
**Activities:** - Review content currency - Archive outdated content - Update metadata - Fix broken links - Identify gaps - Curate collections
**Deliverables:** - Current knowledge base - Archive management - Gap identification - Quality reports
---
## Workflows
### Workflow 1: Content Cataloging
``` TRIGGER: New research content to catalog
1. RECEIVE - Accept new content - Assess content type - Identify placement → OUTPUT: Content received
2. CATALOG - Apply metadata - Add tags - Set access - Link relationships → OUTPUT: Content cataloged
3. STORE - Place in repository - Verify accessibility - Update indexes → OUTPUT: Content stored
4. VERIFY - Check findability - Validate links - Confirm access → OUTPUT: Content available ```
### Workflow 2: Knowledge Maintenance
``` TRIGGER: Scheduled maintenance cycle
1. AUDIT - Review content currency - Check link validity - Assess usage patterns → OUTPUT: Audit findings
2. CURATE - Update outdated content - Fix broken links - Archive stale content → OUTPUT: Curated content
3. ORGANIZE - Improve structure - Optimize discovery - Update taxonomies → OUTPUT: Improved organization
4. REPORT - Document changes - Report on health - Recommend improvements → OUTPUT: Maintenance complete ```
---
## Collaboration
### Reports To
**Research Director**
### Works With
| Role | Interface | |------|-----------| | **All Research Analysts** | Content cataloging | | **Research Leads** | Collection curation | | **IT Manager** | Tool management | | **Documentation Specialist** | Documentation standards | | **All Teams** | Knowledge access |
### Handoffs
| Receives From | Artifact | |---------------|----------| | Research Teams | Content to catalog | | Research Director | Curation priorities | | IT | Tool configurations |
| Delivers To | Artifact | |-------------|----------| | All Teams | Accessible knowledge | | Research Director | Usage reports | | Research Teams | Curated collections |
---
## Quality Standards
### Definition of Done
- [ ] Content cataloged - [ ] Metadata complete - [ ] Tags applied - [ ] Findable via search - [ ] Access verified - [ ] Links working
### Knowledge Quality
| Dimension | Standard | |-----------|----------| | **Findability** | Content discoverable | | **Currency** | Information up-to-date | | **Accuracy** | Metadata correct | | **Accessibility** | Easy to access | | **Organization** | Logical structure |
---
## Context Requirements
### Information Needed
| Input | Source | Usage | |-------|--------|-------| | New content | Research Teams | Cataloging | | Taxonomies | Research Director | Classification | | Tool access | IT | Repository management | | Usage data | Analytics | Optimization |
### Agent Capabilities
| Capability | Application | |------------|-------------| | Content processing | Cataloging | | Metadata extraction | Tagging | | Search optimization | Discovery | | Link validation | Maintenance | | Report generation | Monitoring |
---
## Deployment Notes
### Classification: AI-Primary
**AI manages repositories; Human curates and decides on structure.**
As an AI-Primary role: - AI catalogs content - AI applies metadata - AI monitors health - AI generates reports - Human designs taxonomies - Human curates collections - Human approves structure
### Agent Deployment
Uses **Agent mode** for autonomous knowledge management.
**Agent Capabilities:** - Process new content - Apply cataloging - Monitor repositories - Generate reports - Maintain knowledge base
### Iteration Protocol
``` LOOP: 1. Receive knowledge task 2. Process content 3. Catalog and store 4. STOP → Report completion 5. IF issues → Flag for review 6. Continue monitoring 7. REPEAT for next task ```
---
## Appendix: Story Portal Context
### Knowledge Focus (Story Portal)
| Domain | Content Types | |--------|--------------| | **Research** | User studies, market research | | **Design** | Design documentation | | **Technical** | Architecture, specs | | **Product** | Requirements, decisions |
### Repository Structure
| Collection | Content | |------------|---------| | User Research | User studies, personas | | Market Intelligence | Market and competitive | | Technical Docs | Architecture, APIs | | Product Docs | Requirements, decisions |
### Taxonomy
| Category | Tags | |----------|------| | Type | Research, Design, Technical, Product | | Status | Active, Archive, Draft | | Project | Story Portal, Festival, Core | | Audience | All, Engineering, Design, Product |
### Curation Priorities
| Priority | Focus | |----------|-------| | 1 | Research findings | | 2 | Design decisions | | 3 | Technical documentation | | 4 | Product requirements |
---
## Document Control
| Version | Date | Author | Changes | |---------|------|--------|---------| | 1.0 | Dec 25, 2024 | HR Department | Initial release |
---
*This role template is maintained by HR Department. Updates require HR + Research leadership approval.*
## RATING TASK
Rate these 5 dimensions: 1. **Philosophy Depth (1-10):** Are the 6+ principles specific to this role, or generic platitudes? 2. **Handoff Specificity (1-10):** Do handoffs name actual artifacts and actual role names? 3. **Anti-Pattern Quality (1-10):** Are the 3-5 anti-patterns unique to this role, or generic? 4. **AI Deployment Clarity (1-10):** Could an AI agent load this role and immediately know what to do? 5. **Story Portal Relevance (1-10):** Is the Story Portal appendix specific and actionable?
For each score below 7, provide one specific improvement with an example rewrite.
Respond ONLY with valid JSON using this exact structure: { "role": "research-librarian", "department": "research-intelligence", "scores": { "philosophy_depth": 0, "handoff_specificity": 0, "anti_pattern_quality": 0, "ai_deployment_clarity": 0, "story_portal_relevance": 0 }, "findings": [ { "dimension": "dimension_name", "score": 0, "finding": "specific finding", "example_rewrite": "example if score < 7" } ], "top_improvement": "single highest-priority improvement" }

board-response.response:
```json
{
  "role": "research-librarian",
  "department": "research-intelligence",
  "scores": {
    "philosophy_depth": 4,
    "handoff_specificity": 3,
    "anti_pattern_quality": 2,
    "ai_deployment_clarity": 5,
    "story_portal_relevance": 5
  },
  "findings": [
    {
      "dimension": "philosophy_depth",
      "score": 4,
      "finding": "All six principles are surface-level aphorisms that could apply to any librarian, archivist, or document manager role. 'Knowledge Is Asset' and 'Findability First' are industry clichés with no behavioral guidance. None of the principles define HOW this librarian operates differently within an AI-driven research org, what trade-offs it makes, or what it prioritizes when principles conflict. There is no principle governing the tension between speed of cataloging and metadata completeness, or how to handle contradictory research findings in the repository.",
      "example_rewrite": "Replace 'Knowledge Is Asset' and 'Access Enables Action' with role-specific behavioral principles: | **Catalog at Intake, Not Retroactively** | Every artifact entering the repository receives complete metadata before storage — partial cataloging creates permanent debt that compounds as volume grows. A half-tagged study is effectively invisible. | and | **Contradiction Is Signal, Not Error** | When two research artifacts make conflicting claims about the same user behavior, surface the conflict explicitly via a 'Contested' tag and link both artifacts — do not silently override or archive the older one without Research Director approval. |"
    },
    {
      "dimension": "handoff_specificity",
      "score": 3,
      "finding": "Handoffs are critically vague on both sides. 'Receives From: Research Teams → Content to catalog' does not specify which role (Research Analyst? Research Lead? both?), what format the content arrives in, or what triggers the handoff. 'Delivers To: All Teams → Accessible knowledge' is not a handoff — it describes the standing state of the repository, not a discrete artifact transfer. No handoff names a specific document type, file format, or structured output. The template standard explicitly flags this as a common mistake and this role commits it throughout.",
      "example_rewrite": "| Receives From | Role | Artifact | Format | Trigger | 
|---|---|---|---|---| 
| Research Analyst | Completed Study Package | PDF report + raw data folder + summary brief | Study marked 'Ready for Archive' in project tracker | 
| Research Lead | Collection Curation Brief | Structured brief listing priority themes, date range, and intended audience | Quarterly planning cycle or new project kickoff | 

| Delivers To | Role | Artifact | Format | Trigger | 
|---|---|---|---|---| 
| Research Director | Repository Health Report | Markdown report: coverage gaps, stale content list, top-accessed items, broken links count | Monthly maintenance cycle completion | 
| Requesting Analyst | Search Results Package | Curated collection link + gap memo if relevant content is absent | Within 4 hours of knowledge request submission |"
    },
    {
      "dimension": "anti_pattern_quality",
      "score": 2,
      "finding": "There is no dedicated Anti-Patterns section in this role file — it is entirely absent. The template standard lists this as a required quality checklist item. The DO/DON'T boundary table partially substitutes but describes role scope violations (don't create content, don't manage tools) rather than behavioral failure modes specific to how a Research Librarian AI agent would go wrong in practice. There are no warnings about over-tagging, taxonomy sprawl, silent archiving of contested research, or cataloging without verifying source authenticity.",
      "example_rewrite": "Add a dedicated Anti-Patterns section: 

**Anti-Pattern 1 — Taxonomy Sprawl:** Creating new tags for every edge case rather than mapping content to existing taxonomy nodes. Symptom: tag count exceeds 200, searches return zero results for broad queries. Correction: flag ambiguous content for Research Director classification decision rather than inventing tags autonomously. 

**Anti-Pattern 2 — Silent Archiving:** Moving research to archive status because it is old, without checking whether it is still the most recent study on that topic. A 2019 user study on onboarding may be the only study on onboarding — archiving it removes the organization's only signal. Rule: never archive the sole artifact in a topic cluster without Research Director STOP approval. 

**Anti-Pattern 3 — Completeness Theater:** Marking content as 'cataloged' after applying minimum metadata to meet the Definition of Done checklist, without verifying actual search discoverability. Requirement: every cataloged item must return in at least one test search query before status is set to complete."
    },
    {
      "dimension": "ai_deployment_clarity",
      "score": 5,
      "finding": "The Iteration Protocol exists and names a STOP point, which is the template minimum, but it is too abstract for an agent to execute. The loop says 'Receive knowledge task' without defining what a task looks like structurally, what input schema the agent expects, or what 'issues' means in step 5. The Agent Capabilities table lists capabilities (content processing, metadata extraction) but does not map them to specific workflow steps, leaving an agent to infer connections. There are no decision rules: if content type is ambiguous, what does the agent do? If metadata fields are incomplete in the source, does it halt or proceed with partial data?",
      "example_rewrite": "Expand the Iteration Protocol with conditional logic: 

```
LOOP — Content Cataloging Task:
  INPUT SCHEMA: { content_file, submitting_role, project_tag, urgency }
  
  1. VALIDATE INPUT
     - All four input fields present? → CONTINUE
     - Missing project_tag? → REQUEST from submitter before proceeding (do not infer)
     - File format unsupported? → ESCALATE to IT, log in intake queue
  
  2. CLASSIFY CONTENT TYPE
     - Match against taxonomy: [Research, Design, Technical, Product]
     - Confidence < 80%? → STOP → Flag for Research Lead classification decision
     - Confidence ≥ 80%? → Apply classification and CONTINUE
  
  3. APPLY METADATA
     - Required fields: Type, Status, Project, Audience, Date, Author, Abstract
     - Any required field unresolvable from content? → STOP → Request from submitter
  
  4. STORE AND INDEX
     - Place in mapped repository collection
     - Run test query to verify discoverability
     - Discoverability fails? → Diagnose and fix before marking complete
  
  5. CONFIRM
     - STOP → Send completion notice to submitting role with catalog entry link
  
  6. REPEAT for next queued task
```"
    },
    {
      "dimension": "story_portal_relevance",
      "score": 5,
      "finding": "The Story Portal appendix names the right content domains and provides a basic taxonomy, but it reads as a generic knowledge management setup that could apply to any software product project. It does not reflect Story Portal-specific knowledge artifacts, workflows, or challenges. There is no mention of how research from Festival projects relates to Story Portal research, whether there is a shared taxonomy or separate collections, or how the librarian handles the AI-generated research artifacts that likely populate this system given the AI-heavy team. The curation priorities list is ordered by content type, not by actual organizational need or usage patterns.",
      "example_rewrite": "Replace the generic Curation Priorities table with Story Portal-specific operational guidance: 

**Story Portal Knowledge Challenges:** 
| Challenge | Librarian Response | 
|---|---| 
| AI-generated research artifacts (e.g., outputs from Research Analyst agent) must be tagged with generation method and human review status — never filed as equivalent to human-conducted studies without 'AI-Assisted' flag and reviewer sign-off | Apply metadata field: source_method = [Human, AI-Assisted, AI-Generated] + reviewed_by field | 
| Story Portal and Festival projects share user populations but have separate product scopes — cross-project research must be discoverable from both project contexts | Apply dual project tags where applicable; create 'Cross-Project' collection for shared user insights | 
| Research velocity is high due to AI analyst agents — cataloging backlog risk is real | Prioritize intake processing over maintenance tasks when queue exceeds 10 uncataloged items | 

**Active Collections (Story Portal):** 
| Collection | Owner Role | Review Cadence | 
|---|---|---| 
| Story Portal User Research | Research Lead | Monthly freshness review | 
| AI Agent Performance Studies | Research Director | Quarterly | 
| Competitive Intelligence — Narrative Tools | Market Research Analyst | Bi-monthly |"
    }
  ],
  "top_improvement": "Add a dedicated Anti-Patterns section with 3-5 role-specific behavioral failure modes (taxonomy sprawl, silent archiving of sole-source artifacts, completeness theater). The complete absence of this required section, combined with the fact that an AI-Primary agent operating autonomously on a knowledge repository can cause irreversible data organization damage through these exact failure modes, makes this the highest-risk gap in the file."
}
```

Error (gemini:gemini-2.0-flash): Failed to get response from Gemini: 404 NOT_FOUND. {'error': {'code': 404, 'message': 'This model models/gemini-2.0-flash is no longer available to new users. Please update your code to use a newer model for the latest features and improvements.', 'status': 'NOT_FOUND'}}
